mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of non-overlapping periods when FEDFUNDS is above 5%, or Rank IC is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate at or above 4.5 percent signals slackening labor market conditions that precede downward revisions to corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in retail sales and industrial production indicates a synchronized contraction across consumption and manufacturing sectors that amplifies recession risk and drives negative S&P 500 returns beyond the effect of either variable alone.
failure_condition: A regression of 20-day returns on the interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO yields a t-statistic magnitude below 1.5 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Restrictive monetary policy with the federal funds rate above 5 percent and CPI above 200 sustains elevated real discount rates and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS above 5%, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1450 thousand units indicate a contraction in residential investment and mortgage demand that transmits through construction and consumer durable channels to reduce broad earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when HOUST is below 1450.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment above 135 million persons confirms a tight labor market that sustains wage income and aggregate consumption, supporting near-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered weeks when PAYEMS exceeds 135000.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below 115 during a high-inflation environment with CPI above 200 signals stagflationary supply constraints that simultaneously raise input costs and reduce output, amplifying margin compression and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint condition of INDPRO below 115 and CPIAUCSL above 200 fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a logistic specification.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales below 375 billion dollars signal a slowdown in consumer spending that directly pressures top-line revenue growth for consumption-dependent sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when RSAFS falls below 375000.
